summary: Soundboard capture of a wildly energetic 10‑11‑83 MSG show – stellar St. Stephen and China > Rider moments despite fair‑ish tape clarity and a few rough songs.
review: The performance is remembered for its electric second set, with St. Stephen delivering goose‑bumps, a scorching China > Rider segue and strong Throwing Stones, Jack Straw and Birdsong jams.  The sbd source is usable but not crisp – audience bleed is sparse and the mix can sound muddy on some tunes (Wang Dang Doodle, Day Job).  Overall fans rate the show highly, though opinions diverge on a handful of songs.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance (especially St. Stephen and second‑set jams) while noting only moderate tape quality; weighted average of show (≈4.5) and recording (≈3) yields ~4.1.

recording_quality.technical_notes: sbd with limited crowd ambience; some reviewers call it "so‑so" or "not crisp"; occasional muddiness on certain tracks (Wang Dang Doodle, Day Job) but overall listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set erupts with massive crowd excitement; St. Stephen is teased for minutes before breaking out, creating a high‑energy peak.  Transitions are occasionally sloppy (e.g., into Stones), but overall the flow from Miracle to Bertha to China Doll feels natural and thrilling.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised repeatedly for soaring guitar tone, inventive fills on Day Job and strong vocal delivery; a few reviewers note his voice sounding off‑key on certain songs.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass described as "nimble" and a solid groove that underpins the jam sections; occasional mention of Phil’s mic during China Doll.
band_member_comments.Bob: Rhythm guitar called "sizzling" and "out‑of‑this‑world"; some note his level is low in the mix on certain tracks.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) receives high marks for his electronic piano texture, though a few call it "tinny"; overall keyboard work praised on China Doll and Birdsong.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (drummer) gets thumbs‑up from fans, with the drum break before St. Stephen highlighted as a crowd‑pleaser.
